citations.description: Full test file — no semantic fixture tests present; all tests are routing/registration/rubric-text checks.
Held-out edge tests — also no contained/exceeding/contradicting semantic fixture tests.

findings.criterion: Fixture-driven tests: a leaf narrowing its parent PASSES; a leaf adding out-of-parent-scope deliverables fires; a leaf contradicting a parent AC fires.
findings.detail: Neither test_plan_review_g7.py nor test_d4cf_g7_edge.py (the only G7-specific test files) contains any fixture-driven semantic test that exercises the three required scenarios: (1) a leaf that consistently narrows the parent passing, (2) a leaf that adds out-of-scope deliverables firing, and (3) a leaf contradicting a parent AC firing. An exhaustive search over the tests/ directory for terms such as 'contained', 'exceeding', 'contradicting', 'narrowing', 'out-of-parent-scope', 'leaf_narrows', 'leaf_adds', 'leaf_contradicts', and related patterns returns no matches. The tests present cover routing logic (applies() gates), registration, posture, coach-move existence, rubric text assertions, and the promotion-path doc — but none exercise the behavioral semantics of the criterion against fixture parent/leaf pairs.
findings.remediation: Add three parametrized or distinct test functions (e.g. in test_plan_review_g7.py) that use a FakeRunner or equivalent harness to drive the G7 agent through fixture parent/leaf pairs: (1) a leaf that narrows the parent → expect no G7 finding; (2) a leaf that adds a deliverable absent from the parent → expect a G7 finding; (3) a leaf that contradicts a parent AC → expect a G7 finding.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
